title: Old containers created with libpod_parent cgroup won't start with 0.8.2
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:
>
/kind bug
>
> /kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
I had old containers created c. 0.7.4 (systemd-based containers with libpod_parent cgroups). They won't start under 0.8.2.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman start old_container

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
```
unable to start container "test_ruby": container create failed: container_linux.go:336: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:279: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"Invalid unit name 'libpod_parent'\""
: internal libpod error
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container starts normally

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Upgraded 0.7.4 to 0.8.2 from updates-testing

config.json has 

```
"cgroupsPath" : "libpod_parent:libpod:996dc151918c0c591ad92d6bf0aa49d5c05d35a94361baa98ce9250d02008597"
```



**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.8.2.1
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
If you need this containers to keep working, you should hardcode the `cgroupfs` cgroup driver in `/etc/containers/libpod.conf`. If you'd like to migrate to the systemd driver, you'll have to commit any changes in the container and spin up a new container based on the committed image. Podman containers are immutable by design, so we can't swap settings such as CGroup parent for created containers.

We probably should have conveyed that the swap in defaults would potentially cause breakage better in the release notes.

We could potentially provide a `podman container clone` that would allow you to duplicate an existing container (commit the image and use it for the new container, and duplicate the config as well, but allow changes to be made).

+1 for more information in changelog.txt.

I see that `podman --cgroup-manager cgroupfs`  starts these older containers (created when cgroupfs was the default) so not a bug but more like unexpected behaviour.
